topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed, a monotonous soundtrack to my rising panic. I clutched the crumpled paper in my sweaty palm, the ink blurring under my frantic grip. When Professor Albright called my name, the silence in the lecture hall felt like a physical weight pressing down on my chest. Every eye in the room seemed fixed on me, dissecting the evidence they had constructed. I mumbled something incoherent, my throat tight, and retreated to my seat, the cheap plastic chair suddenly feeling like a cage. The accusation hung in the air, sharp and undeniable. I could feel the heat creeping up my neck, a flush that betrayed the tremor in my hands. I just wanted the floor to swallow me whole.

[story 2]

"I don't understand," I managed, the words barely a whisper, my voice cracking mid-sentence. The accusation had landed like a physical blow, shattering the carefully constructed composure I’d maintained all semester. My gaze dropped to the worn floor tiles, tracing patterns in the dust with my eyes. A cold dread pooled in my stomach, making my limbs feel heavy and unresponsive. I could feel the blood draining from my face, leaving a clammy sheen. When the committee members started questioning me, I kept my eyes down, focusing intensely on the texture of my textbook, desperate to disappear into the pages, wishing I could evaporate.

[story 3]

The air in the office was sterile and unforgiving. Every word spoken by the panel felt amplified, each critique a tiny shard of glass digging into my composure. I kept my hands folded tightly in my lap, the knuckles white against the fabric of my jeans. Looking at the evidence laid out before me, a wave of nausea washed over me. My vision swam for a moment, and I instinctively pressed the heels of my hands against my eyes, trying to block out the harsh scrutiny. When the lead professor finished speaking, I simply nodded, a slow, almost imperceptible movement, and offered no further defense, just a profound sense of defeat settling deep within my bones.
